data.narrative:
Synspective is a public-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised approximately $220M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of four same-sector, same-stage peers: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B), and Astroscale ($900M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach, given the availability of direct valuation comparables across identical stage and sector criteria. This method yields a median implied valuation of $6.7B, with the peer set establishing a wide range from $900M at the low end (Astroscale) to $37.0B at the high end (Rocket Lab). The public-comp anchoring method, which applies a P/S multiple from six public Space & Aerospace tickers (median 59.8x), cannot be applied without disclosure of Synspective's current revenue.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5.7B to $7.6B, centered on $6.7B. This range reflects the dispersion within a deliberately narrow peer set and accounts for methodological conservatism in the absence of revenue-based anchors. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, unit economics relative to peers, and the significant valuation spread among comparables—a 41-fold difference between Astroscale and Rocket Lab—which suggests sector-wide heterogeneity in growth expectations and profitability profiles that single-point estimates do not capture.
